Welcome Raman, tell me about your journey from your hometown to this SSB center and how you have spent your time since arriving yesterday.
Give me a brief overview of your daily routine as an 18-year-old who has just completed the 12th standard.
How have you prepared yourself for this NDA entry, and what specific qualities do you think you possess that make you a fit for the Armed Forces?
How do your parents influence your decision-making, especially regarding your career choice in the military?
Describe your relationship with your siblings and how you support each other in your respective goals.
If I were to ask your father about one area where you need to improve, what would he say?

Tell me about your father's and mother's occupation, your relationship with each of them, the number of siblings you have and what they are currently doing, your family's monthly income, and your specific responsibilities at home.
Looking back at your 12th-grade journey, which subjects did you find most engaging and why?
Tell me about a project or a specific academic challenge you faced in school and how you overcame it.
Why did you choose your specific stream in 11th and 12th, and how does it align with your future goals?

Starting from your 10th standard, tell me the names of your schools, your percentage in 10th and 12th, your favorite and least favorite subjects, the names of your three best friends, and any other competitive exams you have appeared for.
Have you ever held any formal position of responsibility, such as a school prefect or house captain, during your school years?
Describe a time you had to take charge of a situation at home when your parents were away.
How do you balance your academic commitments with your interests in running and painting?
What do you consider your most significant achievement in school, whether it was in academics or extra-curricular activities?
Tell me about a time your painting was recognized or appreciated by your teachers or peers.
Have you ever won any awards or certificates in running competitions, and what did that experience teach you?
Tell me about a time you set a target for your 12th-grade marks but failed to achieve it; how did you react?
Describe a situation where you lost a race or a sports event despite your best efforts.
Have you ever faced a creative block in your hobby of painting, and how did you handle the frustration of not being able to produce your best work?
If you were tasked with organizing a school-wide painting exhibition, what steps would you take to ensure its success?
Give me an example of a time you motivated a friend or a teammate who wanted to give up during a physical activity.
How do you handle disagreements within a group when you are working on a shared task or project?
What medium of painting do you prefer to work with, and what themes do you usually try to capture in your art?
Since you enjoy running, tell me about your best timing for a 5km run and the training schedule you follow to maintain your stamina.
How has painting helped in developing your patience and attention to detail, and how will this benefit you as an officer?
What was the specific moment or event that inspired you to choose the NDA over a regular graduation degree?
If you are not selected in this attempt, what is your alternative plan for your career and education?
Which arm or service of the Indian Army interests you the most, and what have you done to learn more about it?
What is your opinion on the 'Agnipath' scheme and its impact on the youth of our country?
Can you name three major defense deals India has signed in the last year and explain their significance?
Tell me about any recent international event that you feel has a direct impact on India's national security.
While going for your NDA written exam, you see a person injured in a hit-and-run case with no one around. What would you do?
You realize that your painting for a major competition has been damaged by a classmate just before the submission. How do you handle the situation?
During a group trek, one of your friends develops a severe leg cramp and you are still 5 kilometers away from the camp. What is your course of action?
How do you think your stay here at the SSB has changed your perspective on leadership and teamwork?
Is there anything mentioned in your PIQ that we haven't discussed, but you would like to share with me?
Do you have any questions for me or any feedback on the way this interview was conducted?
Tanmay, this is your 9th attempt at the SSB; talk me through how your preparation strategy has evolved from your first attempt to this one.
Briefly introduce yourself, highlighting your journey from completing your B.Tech to deciding to join the Coast Guard as a combatant.
Since you are 26 and working, how do you manage to maintain the physical standards required for the forces alongside your professional life?
How has your family's perspective on your career choice changed over these nine attempts?
Describe a situation where you had a significant disagreement with your parents and how you reached a middle ground.
Being 26, what are the specific financial and social responsibilities you currently fulfill within your household?

Tell me your father's and mother's occupation, your relationship with each of them, who you are closer to in the family, your siblings' current activities, the total family income, and the specific household chores you handle.
Why did you choose B.Tech as your graduation stream, and how do you think a technical background will help you in the Coast Guard?
Looking at your academic record, your marks seem to have fluctuated; what were the reasons for this inconsistency?
Tell me about two subjects from your engineering curriculum that you disliked the most and how you managed to clear them.

Tell me about your schooling from 10th grade onwards, the marks obtained in each stage, your favorite and least favorite subjects, the names of two teachers who influenced you the most, any scholarships you won, and the extracurricular activities you were involved in during college.
As a senior member of your volleyball team, what administrative responsibilities do you handle beyond just playing the game?
Can you give me an instance where you took an initiative at your workplace or home that resulted in a positive change?
How do you prioritize your daily tasks to ensure your hobby of jogging doesn't interfere with your professional or family commitments?
What do you consider your most significant achievement in Volleyball, and what did it take to reach that level?
Aside from your academic degree, what is one skill you have mastered independently that you are proud of?
Tell me about a time you received recognition or an award during your B.Tech projects or internships.
You've faced 8 previous rejections; what is the one specific personality trait you identified as a reason for not being recommended earlier?
Describe a time you failed to lead your team to victory in a sports event and how you handled the post-match debrief with your teammates.
Tell me about a technical project in college that didn't go as planned and what your fallback strategy was.
In a high-pressure Volleyball match, if you see your teammates losing focus, how do you step in to restore their morale?
Describe a situation where you had to lead a group of people who were more experienced or older than you.
What specific leadership qualities have you observed in your current superiors that you aim to emulate in the Coast Guard?
You mentioned jogging; what is your current 5km timing, and how do you track your progress and improve your stamina?
If you were asked to organize a state-level Volleyball tournament, what would be your step-by-step organizational plan?
How do you keep yourself updated with the latest changes in the rules and international standards of Volleyball?
With a B.Tech degree, you have multiple career options; why are you specifically determined to join the Coast Guard as a combatant?
What specific aspects of the Coast Guard's 'Vayam Rakshamah' motto resonate with your personal values?
If you are not selected in this 9th attempt, what is your concrete plan for your career and personal growth moving forward?
What is your take on the current maritime security challenges India faces in the Arabian Sea, especially concerning piracy and drone attacks?
Can you explain the significance of the Agnipath scheme and its long-term impact on the operational readiness of the Indian Armed Forces?
Discuss the recent developments in the Indo-Pacific region and how they affect the role of the Indian Coast Guard.
While patrolling, you find a foreign vessel in Indian waters with a medical emergency but no clear identification; what will be your immediate course of action?
You are leading a team for a marathon, and your best runner suffers an injury 2km before the finish line; how do you handle the situation?
If you discover a colleague is consistently neglecting their duties which puts the team at risk, how would you address this without involving the seniors immediately?
Looking back at this interview, which response of yours do you think was the most honest and which one could have been better?
Is there anything specific in your PIQ that you were prepared to talk about but I haven't asked you yet?
Rate your own performance in this interview on a scale of 1 to 10 and justify why you deserve that rating.
